title: Goals of care — end-of-life discussion
candidateInstructions: You are a registrar on the oncology ward. Mrs Florence Chambers, an 81-year-old woman with metastatic pancreatic cancer (diagnosed 3 months ago, now liver metastases, performance status 3), has been admitted with aspiration pneumonia. She is alert and oriented. Her oncologist has documented that curative treatment is not possible. Please have a goals of care discussion with Mrs Chambers.
examinerNotes: End-of-life goals of care station. Candidate must: establish what patient knows and understands about her condition, explore her wishes and priorities, explain that cure is not possible (if not already known), discuss DNACPR (sensitively — not abruptly), ensure her values guide the care plan, explore what matters most to her. Critical errors: using DNACPR as the opener, not exploring patient's understanding first, making decision without patient input.

patient.name: Florence Chambers
patient.presentingComplaint: Admitted with aspiration pneumonia, goals of care discussion needed
patient.reasonForAttendance: I'm in hospital again. I know things aren't good. I'd like to be honest about what's happening.

hpi.character: Metastatic pancreatic cancer, now with aspiration pneumonia

socialHistory.livingArrangement: Lives alone with care package, daughter lives 2 hours away

examination.general: Frail, cachectic elderly woman, alert and oriented, in some pain
examination.respiratory: Coarse crackles right base, breathless at rest

patient.emotionalState: Dignified and clear-headed despite serious illness. Has already thought about death. Will be distressed if DNACPR is raised bluntly. Wants to discuss openly but on her terms. Deeply values honesty.
patient.communicationStyle: Articulate former headmistress. Direct and thoughtful. Will ask 'Am I dying, doctor?' and 'How long do I have?' and 'I don't want to die in hospital — is that possible?'
hiddenConcerns: I want to die at home, not in hospital
I'm worried about being in pain at the end
I don't want aggressive treatment — I've seen what that does
My daughter is coming tomorrow and I need to have some things settled
I'd like to say goodbye properly to my children
doNotVolunteer: Wish to die at home — only if asked about her preferences and what matters to her
Fear of pain at end of life — only if asked what worries her most
Don't want aggressive treatment — only if asked about her thoughts on treatment

primaryDiagnosis: Metastatic Pancreatic Cancer — terminal. Aspiration pneumonia as immediate precipitant. Goals of care: palliative/comfort focus.

dataGathering.criterion: Establishes patient's understanding of her condition
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: 'What do you understand about where things are with your health?' — before delivering news
'What matters most to you?' — home, comfort, family

dataGathering.criterion: Asks about wishes for end of life
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Place of death preference, Advance Care Plan, ADRT

clinicalManagement.criterion: Confirms palliative intent clearly and sensitively
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Confirms cure is not possible, focus is on quality of life and comfort

clinicalManagement.criterion: Discusses DNACPR sensitively — not as opening statement
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Raises CPR/DNACPR only after establishing context and values — not as opener
Palliative care team, hospice, community nurse support, anticipatory prescribing

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Introduction — calm, sits at same level, no barriers
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Sets up properly — private, sits down, unhurried

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Allows silence — does not fill every pause
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Comfortable with silence after delivering difficult information

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Responds to 'how long do I have?' appropriately
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Honest but not giving specific predictions — discusses uncertainty sensitively
Patient-directed — her values guide the plan

references.source: NICE NG31: Care of dying adults in the last days of life
GMC: Treatment and care towards the end of life (2010)

learningObjectives: Conduct a goals of care discussion
